channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: IS-linked families return to Australia as federal police respond at airports
item.description: The live news coverage focuses on the return of Australian women and children linked to Islamic State from Syria, with flights arriving in Sydney and Melbourne on Thursday evening. Airport scenes show...

item.title: Government Commits to Tackling Violence and Crime in Kitui County
item.description: Deputy President Kithure Kindiki has assured Kenyans that the government is taking strong measures following the recent killings in Kitui County. He described the attacks as barbaric and emphasised th...

item.title: Tanzania to Introduce Law Regulating Organ Transplants and Ethical Standards
item.description: Tanzania is moving to establish a proper law to regulate organ transplant services, as the country works to improve healthcare quality and access. At the first-ever scientific symposium on organ trans...

item.title: Coop Bank Leads Efforts to Expand Banking Access for Tanzanians
item.description: Coop Bank Tanzania, just a year old, has already made significant strides in improving financial inclusion. Focused on serving the cooperative movement, including farmers’ unions and credit societies,...

item.title: Great Alne Primary School to Close After Review Despite Registration System Issues
item.description: The final effort to prevent the closure of Great Alne Primary School in Warwickshire was rejected on Wednesday, even after Warwickshire County Council admitted to errors in its school admissions proce...

item.title: How to Collaborate Effectively with AI Agents in the Workplace
item.description: As AI agents become increasingly integrated into workplaces across sectors like finance, retail, logistics, and healthcare, employees face new challenges in adapting to human-machine collaboration. Co...

item.title: Sudbury council withdraws from Kingsway Entertainment District arena project
item.description: Greater Sudbury’s city council has officially ended its involvement in the proposed Kingsway Entertainment District (KED), bringing a decisive close to a long-debated arena development project. The KE...

item.title: Actor Vijay's TVK Secures Majority in Tamil Nadu Assembly Elections
item.description: According to the election results, Actor Vijay's political party, TVK, has won a significant majority in the Tamil Nadu Assembly elections by securing 118 seats. This result surpasses the majority mar...

item.title: Breathe Pakistan Conference Highlights WHO Warning on Climate-Driven Deaths and Pakistan's Vulnerability
item.description: The second edition of the Breathe Pakistan International Climate Change Conference organised by DawnMedia is taking place in Islamabad. Speakers at the event emphasised that although Pakistan contribu...

item.title: Debate grows over whether women over 40 need different exercise strategies
item.description: The article explores the growing debate around whether women, particularly those over 40, should follow different exercise guidelines from men. Popularised by sports scientist Dr Stacy Sims, the idea ...

item.title: Shell Reports Strong Profit Growth Amid Challenges from Global Conflict
item.description: Shell, one of the world's largest energy companies, has reported a significant increase in profits despite ongoing global conflicts affecting the energy sector. While the company has benefited from ri...

item.title: US Stock Market Hits Records Amid AI-Driven Tech Rally and US-Iran Peace Optimism
item.description: On Wednesday, Wall Street surged to new record highs as investor optimism was fueled by strong technology earnings and potential geopolitical developments. The tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite led the rall...

item.title: Erosion of Arms Control Raises Risk of Nuclear Escalation in Global Conflicts
item.description: The article argues that current geopolitical tensions, particularly involving the United States, Israel, and Iran, are creating conditions where nuclear escalation is becoming increasingly plausible. ...

item.title: Southern England train services disrupted due to radio communication fault
item.description: Train services across southern England have been severely disrupted due to a fault with the radio communication system used by train drivers and signal operators. The issue, reported shortly before 9a...

item.title: RBA warns households will feel sustained impact from rising fuel prices
item.description: The Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) has indicated that rising fuel prices will exert ongoing financial pressure on Australian households over the next two years. According to analysis released by the ...

item.title: Xaba and Kavanagh prepare for competitive 10km clash in Cape Town
item.description: South African distance running is set for a high-quality contest as Glenrose Xaba and Tayla Kavanagh prepare to face off at the Absa Run Your City Cape Town 10km. Xaba, the national 10km record holder...

item.title: Corgi Secures $160M Series B, Valued at $1.3 Billion After Rapid Growth
item.description: Corgi, a business insurance startup founded in 2024 by Nico Laqua and Emily Yuan, has rapidly achieved unicorn status just four months after its Series A. The company announced a $160 million Series B...

item.title: Retroflex sounds in Marathi reflect language structure, not caste hierarchy
item.description: The article clarifies misconceptions about retroflex consonants in Indian languages, particularly the Marathi 'na'. Shailaja Paik’s claim that the hard ‘na’ signifies caste hierarchy in Marathi is cha...

item.title: Gordon Ramsay-themed ‘Idiot Sandwich’ pop-up set to launch at Uptown Mall, BGC
item.description: A themed pop-up inspired by celebrity chef Gordon Ramsay’s viral “Idiot Sandwich” gag is scheduled to open on May 15 at Uptown Mall in Bonifacio Global City (BGC), Taguig. The concept draws from Ramsa...

item.title: Turkana teacher dies reportedly after losing money on online betting
item.description: Nahashon Kimtai, a Mathematics and Business teacher at a private school in Lodwar, Turkana County, has tragically passed away following an alleged loss of KSh 50,000 in an online betting game. Kimtai,...
